explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: A court filing details lawsuits and regulatory scrutiny related to AI pricing tools for antitrust violations.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This court filing, "Emerging Antitrust Implications of AI for the Hospitality Industry," provides evidence for **external_accountability**, **governance**, and **privacy**. It supports external accountability by detailing lawsuits and regulatory scrutiny of AI pricing tools for antitrust violations. The filing also highlights governance concerns through discussions of settlement terms imposing requirements on software use and mentions intergovernmental bodies researching algorithmic pricing's impact. Finally, it touches on privacy by referencing settlement terms that address data sensitivity in relation to software price recommendations.
relevant_sources.title: Emerging Antitrust Implications of AI for the Hospitality Industry

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A court filing highlights governance concerns by discussing settlement terms that impose requirements on software use. It also mentions intergovernmental bodies researching the impact of algorithmic pricing.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: Settlement terms reference data sensitivity concerning software price recommendations.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Based on 11 publicly available sources, Caesars Entertainment's published materials address 3 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Privacy is addressed through settlement terms that reference data sensitivity in relation to software price recommendations. Additionally, a court filing highlights governance concerns through discussions of settlement terms that impose requirements on software use, and details regulatory scrutiny of AI pricing tools for antitrust violations under external accountability. No qualifying public evidence was found for transparency, fairness, and 2 additional pillars.
